{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Audit Officer",
  "description": "Proforce Limited was incorporated in 2008 as a total defence solution provider specializing in armoured vehicles and personal protection. The company&rsquo;s focus is on the delivery of confidence in protective mobility. We have developed a vibrant market for security and mobile protective products within Nigeria and Africa. PROFORCE has exploited its deep technical know-how to produce outstanding armored products for Government Agencies, Corporate Bodies, Diplomatic Communities and Private individuals within Nigeria and Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Audit Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Internal Audit Officer will be responsible for evaluating and improving the effectiveness of risk management, control, and governance processes within the organization.
	The role involves conducting audits of financial, operational, and compliance activities to ensure efficiency, integrity, and compliance with company policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan, execute, and report on internal audit assignments.
	Assess the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls and risk management systems.
	Review financial records, operational processes, and compliance with policies/procedures.
	Identify gaps, weaknesses, or potential risks in systems and make recommendations.
	Prepare accurate, clear, and concise audit reports for management.
	Monitor follow-up actions on audit recommendations.
	Support external auditors by providing required documentation.
	Conduct investigations on fraud, irregularities, or mismanagement when required.
	Promote a culture of accountability, compliance, and continuous improvement.


Job Requirements
Education &amp; Qualifications:


	B.Sc. / HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field.
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, or CIA is an added advantage.


Experience:


	2&ndash;4 years of proven experience in auditing, internal control, or compliance.
	Hands-on experience in using accounting/audit software.
	Knowledge of auditing standards and risk management practices.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong analytical and investigative skills.
	Excellent knowledge of internal control systems and audit methodology.
	Good communication and report writing skills.
	High attention to detail and accuracy.
	Integrity, confidentiality, and professionalism.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.


Salary
N300,000- N400,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;adebanji@proforcedefence.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-13",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Internal Audit Officer Job at Proforce Limited




        
    

        
        Proforce Limited was incorporated in 2008 as a total defence solution provider specializing in armoured vehicles and personal protection. The company’s focus is on the delivery of confidence in protective mobility. We have developed a vibrant market for security and mobile protective products within Nigeria and Africa. PROFORCE has exploited its deep technical know-how to produce outstanding armored products for Government Agencies, Corporate Bodies, Diplomatic Communities and Private individuals within Nigeria and Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Audit Officer

Job Location: Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Internal Audit Officer will be responsible for evaluating and improving the effectiveness of risk management, control, and governance processes within the organization.
	The role involves conducting audits of financial, operational, and compliance activities to ensure efficiency, integrity, and compliance with company policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan, execute, and report on internal audit assignments.
	Assess the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls and risk management systems.
	Review financial records, operational processes, and compliance with policies/procedures.
	Identify gaps, weaknesses, or potential risks in systems and make recommendations.
	Prepare accurate, clear, and concise audit reports for management.
	Monitor follow-up actions on audit recommendations.
	Support external auditors by providing required documentation.
	Conduct investigations on fraud, irregularities, or mismanagement when required.
	Promote a culture of accountability, compliance, and continuous improvement.


Job Requirements
Education & Qualifications:


	B.Sc. / HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field.
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, or CIA is an added advantage.


Experience:


	2–4 years of proven experience in auditing, internal control, or compliance.
	Hands-on experience in using accounting/audit software.
	Knowledge of auditing standards and risk management practices.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong analytical and investigative skills.
	Excellent knowledge of internal control systems and audit methodology.
	Good communication and report writing skills.
	High attention to detail and accuracy.
	Integrity, confidentiality, and professionalism.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.


Salary
N300,000- N400,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Restaurant Manager",
  "description": "At Genesis Group, we`&#39;`ve been living our dreams and redefining the world of hospitality for over three decades. Our journey has been characterized by excellence in a wide range of services, including Catering, Hotels, Restaurants, and Cinemas. With a team of over 10,000 dedicated professionals and 30 locations spanning across Nigeria and beyond, we take pride in delivering exceptional experiences to our valued customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Manager

Job Locations:&nbsp;Ikeja and Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Manage day-to-day operations of the restaurant, including opening and closing procedures, staff scheduling, and shift management.
	Supervise and coordinate activities of restaurant staff, including servers, chefs, cooks, and support staff, to ensure efficient workflow and high-quality service.
	Train, motivate, and mentor staff, providing guidance, coaching, and performance feedback to foster a positive work environment and promote employee development.
	Monitor food preparation, presentation, and quality standards, ensuring compliance with recipes, portion sizes, and food safety regulations.
	Maintain inventory levels, order supplies, and manage food and beverage costs to optimize profitability and minimize waste and shrinkage.
	Develop and implement operational policies, procedures, and standards to maintain consistency, efficiency, and compliance with health and safety regulations.
	Handle customer inquiries, complaints, and feedback in a prompt and professional manner, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction.
	Plan and execute marketing and promotional activities to attract customers, increase foot traffic, and drive sales growth.
	Monitor sales performance, analyze financial data, and prepare reports to track key performance indicators (KPIs) and identify opportunities for improvement.
	Collaborate with the kitchen team to develop menus, specials, and promotions that meet customer preferences and market trends.
	Ensure cleanliness, sanitation, and maintenance of the restaurant premises, equipment, and facilities, adhering to health and safety standards.
	Implement cost-control measures, such as portion control, inventory management, and labor optimization, to maximize profitability and minimize expenses.
	Build and maintain relationships with suppliers, vendors, and service providers, negotiating contracts, pricing, and terms to secure favorable arrangements.
	Stay updated on industry trends, consumer preferences, and competitive landscape, conducting market research and competitor analysis to inform business decisions.
	Uphold company policies, procedures, and values, promoting a positive brand image and culture of excellence among staff and customers.


Requirements and Qualifications


	High School Diploma or equivalent; Bachelor&#39;s Degree in hospitality management or related field is preferred.
	Proven experience in restaurant management or a similar role, with a strong understanding of restaurant operations, food service standards, and customer service principles.
	Excellent leadership and interpersonal skills, with the ability to motivate and inspire teams to achieve performance targets and deliver exceptional service.
	Knowledge of food safety regulations, health codes, and sanitation practices, with certification in food handling and safety (e.g., ServSafe) preferred.
	Strong communication and conflict resolution abilities, with the capacity to handle customer inquiries and complaints in a calm and professional manner.
	Financial acumen and budget management skills, with the ability to analyze financial data, control costs, and drive revenue growth.
	Organizational and multitasking abilities, with the capacity to prioritize tasks, manage time effectively, and handle multiple responsibilities in a fast-paced environment.
	Flexibility and adaptability to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as required to meet operational needs and accommodate customer demand.
	Proficiency in restaurant management software, POS systems, and Microsoft Office suite.
	Commitment to upholding quality standards, service excellence, and customer satisfaction.


Required Skills:


	Leadership
	Communication
	Customer service
	Team management
	Financial management
	Food safety
	Problem-solving
	Time management
	Marketing
	Adaptability.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;vacancies@genesisgroupng.com&nbsp;using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-24",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-08",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "name": "Genesis Group"
    
  },

Restaurant Manager Job at  Genesis Group




        
    

        
        At Genesis Group, we`'`ve been living our dreams and redefining the world of hospitality for over three decades. Our journey has been characterized by excellence in a wide range of services, including Catering, Hotels, Restaurants, and Cinemas. With a team of over 10,000 dedicated professionals and 30 locations spanning across Nigeria and beyond, we take pride in delivering exceptional experiences to our valued customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Manager

Job Locations: Ikeja and Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage day-to-day operations of the restaurant, including opening and closing procedures, staff scheduling, and shift management.
	Supervise and coordinate activities of restaurant staff, including servers, chefs, cooks, and support staff, to ensure efficient workflow and high-quality service.
	Train, motivate, and mentor staff, providing guidance, coaching, and performance feedback to foster a positive work environment and promote employee development.
	Monitor food preparation, presentation, and quality standards, ensuring compliance with recipes, portion sizes, and food safety regulations.
	Maintain inventory levels, order supplies, and manage food and beverage costs to optimize profitability and minimize waste and shrinkage.
	Develop and implement operational policies, procedures, and standards to maintain consistency, efficiency, and compliance with health and safety regulations.
	Handle customer inquiries, complaints, and feedback in a prompt and professional manner, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction.
	Plan and execute marketing and promotional activities to attract customers, increase foot traffic, and drive sales growth.
	Monitor sales performance, analyze financial data, and prepare reports to track key performance indicators (KPIs) and identify opportunities for improvement.
	Collaborate with the kitchen team to develop menus, specials, and promotions that meet customer preferences and market trends.
	Ensure cleanliness, sanitation, and maintenance of the restaurant premises, equipment, and facilities, adhering to health and safety standards.
	Implement cost-control measures, such as portion control, inventory management, and labor optimization, to maximize profitability and minimize expenses.
	Build and maintain relationships with suppliers, vendors, and service providers, negotiating contracts, pricing, and terms to secure favorable arrangements.
	Stay updated on industry trends, consumer preferences, and competitive landscape, conducting market research and competitor analysis to inform business decisions.
	Uphold company policies, procedures, and values, promoting a positive brand image and culture of excellence among staff and customers.


Requirements and Qualifications


	High School Diploma or equivalent; Bachelor's Degree in hospitality management or related field is preferred.
	Proven experience in restaurant management or a similar role, with a strong understanding of restaurant operations, food service standards, and customer service principles.
	Excellent leadership and interpersonal skills, with the ability to motivate and inspire teams to achieve performance targets and deliver exceptional service.
	Knowledge of food safety regulations, health codes, and sanitation practices, with certification in food handling and safety (e.g., ServSafe) preferred.
	Strong communication and conflict resolution abilities, with the capacity to handle customer inquiries and complaints in a calm and professional manner.
	Financial acumen and budget management skills, with the ability to analyze financial data, control costs, and drive revenue growth.
	Organizational and multitasking abilities, with the capacity to prioritize tasks, manage time effectively, and handle multiple responsibilities in a fast-paced environment.
	Flexibility and adaptability to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as required to meet operational needs and accommodate customer demand.
	Proficiency in restaurant management software, POS systems, and Microsoft Office suite.
	Commitment to upholding quality standards, service excellence, and customer satisfaction.


Required Skills:


	Leadership
	Communication
	Customer service
	Team management
	Financial management
	Food safety
	Problem-solving
	Time management
	Marketing
	Adaptability.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the mail.

title: Job Openings at WTS Energy
content:
WTS Energy provides recruitment and manpower services for the global oil and gas and energy industries. We supply engineers and consultants to our clients' projects and operations, and perform employment outsourcing services such as workforce management in oil and gas regions around the world. WTS Energy operates globally with offices in 14 countries and is operational in over 50 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Lead, Power & Generation Services

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Unit/Division: Power & Drives

Client Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client is rapidly developing an international presence and a growing reputation in the global market, as a key player in Energy Services.


Job Summary


	The successful candidate will report to the General Manager, Power and Drives Division, and will manage the Power Generation Services business of the Group. He is expected to have a strong understanding of the clientele base of the Power Generation Industry with their respective Turbomachinery Assets.
	A keen knowledge of GE Heavy Frame Gas Turbines with strong sales and service experience is an added advantage. The candidate will manage the sales pipeline, identify new opportunities cross-functionally with the Customer Relationship Executives.
	Capable of using the ERRC Grid Model as an effective sales management tool. Other ERP software experience is a clincher.
	Will also be the Account Manager of the Group’s OEM in charge of turbomachinery aftermarket sales/services.
	A strong negotiation and presentation skill is a success matrix for this role. Summarily, he/she will drive technical sales of turbomachinery aftermarket sales/services for the business.


Responsibilities


	Manage and Expand Existing Power Generation Account Business
	Maximize Cross-Selling, Re-Selling Opportunities across the division
	Responsible for the Quick Sales Intelligence (QSI) management and driving to actualize the set sales target or Annual Operating Plan (AOP)
	Ensure coordination of the OEM’s weekly alignment meetings
	Responsible for creating the Project Identification Note (PIN) for identified leads and projects as a guide for business decision making
	In charge of strategic local partnerships for the Power generation business by creating the respective Business Model Canvas (BMC) and Partnership Onboarding Plan (POP) for any potential local partnerships
	Will be responsible for building the quote and proposal for the Power generation business with keen understanding of respective mark-up structure to maximize business profitability
	Will be responsible for customer/client meetings and make virtual and live presentations based on the recipient’s request.
	Create a Sales Lifetime Value (SLV) of at least 2 Customers in a year through structured contract service agreements and aftermarket sales/services support.
	Will be involved in asset management audits and inspections in customer power plants


 Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree or its equivalent in Electrical, Mechanical, Instrument/Controls or Power Engineering
	10 – 15 years of experience in power plant sales and operations especially with the gas turbines and auxiliaries, with at least 3 – 5 years’ experience as a lead, supervisor or manager
	A trained Certification (by any Equipment OEM or Recognized Training Institute) is an advantage


Skills:


	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and at least two (2) of the ERP Sales Software (e.g. Mondays.com, Sales Force, Oracle E-Business, SAP, CMMS etc.)
	Expertise in Energy Management Softwares (EMS) and Root Cause Analysis is an added advantage
	Good Organizational and Synergistic Skills.
	Able to take on more responsibilities upon business change management and exigencies
	Great Technical and Analytical Skills, Reporting Style.


Click here to apply online

 

2.) Talent Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Objective


	The Talent Manager is responsible for designing and delivering integrated talent strategies that support performance excellence, career development, leadership readiness, and organizational effectiveness.
	This role leads performance management, learning and development, recruitment, succession planning, and organizational development initiatives to ensure a high-performing and future-ready workforce aligned with business and operational objectives.


Job Responsibilties


	Monitor and promote the organization’s culture to ensure it reflects the values and ethos.
	Design, implement, and continuously improve the company’s performance management framework.
	Forecast and manage the HR annual budget, encompassing HR services, employee recognition, and development initiatives.
	Support managers in providing ongoing feedback, coaching, and performance improvement plans.
	Partner with leaders to ensure fair, consistent, and effective performance evaluations
	Use performance data to inform development, rewards, succession, and workforce decisions.
	Oversee the training strategy, identifying and implementing a range of learning opportunities, including in-house, virtual, and classroom training, as well as a comprehensive onboarding process for new hires.
	Evaluate talent using the 9-box grid framework, ensuring competencies align with defined job roles and organizational needs.
	Develop career frameworks, competency models, and progression pathways
	Facilitate talent reviews, monitor individual development plans aligned with performance outcomes and partner with leaders and employees on career planning and internal mobility.
	Lead strategic recruitment and workforce planning aligned with business needs
	Support employer branding and candidate experience initiatives
	Manage relationships with external recruiters, training providers, and institutions
	Develop and maintain succession plans for leadership and critical roles
	Assess readiness and bench strength using performance and potential data
	Align succession plans with development and learning strategies
	Support organizational design, role clarity, and structural effectiveness
	Lead or support change management initiatives related to growth or transformation
	Drive culture, engagement, and capability-building initiatives
	Leverage engagement and performance data to inform OD interventions
	Ensure alignment with company policies, labour regulations, and industry standards
	Manage or support talent systems (performance, learning, and talent management platforms)
	Track and report key talent metrics (performance distribution, turnover, internal mobility, readiness)
	Continuously improve talent processes based on insights and business feedback.


Self-Development:


	Continuously and actively engage in learning and development activities to improve competence in functional areas.
	Build strong interpersonal skills for effective relationships with stakeholders at all levels.
	Capacity to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities.


Qualifications / Experience Required


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Organizational Development, Business, or related fields
	Master’s degree in related fields
	Membership of a related professional body e.g. CIPM, PHRI, SHRM etc
	Minimum of 10 years HR experience. 6+ years in talent management, performance management, Learning & Development, or Organizational Design roles
	Experience supporting technical or operational workforces(energy, industrial, or services preferred industries)
	Strong consulting, facilitation, and stakeholder-management skills.


Key Competencies Requirements
Functional / Technical:


	Performance management framework design and rollout experience
	Succession planning and high-potential program expertise
	Change management capability
	Data-driven talent and performance decision-making
	Experience with HRIS, LMS, and talent management systems.


Behavioural:


	Good communication skills
	Helpful and personable attitude with evident desire to assist all employees to perform effectively
	Organized and efficient; able to prioritize work with minimal or no supervision
	Ability to multitask and manage conflicting deadlines and tight schedules
	Must exhibit high level of personal and professional integrity
	Good Interpersonal Skills
	Maturity, tact and including the ability to relate with different categories of people
	Flexibility and willingness to work beyond strict job requirements
	An enterprising attitude that is quick to search out alternative solutions to needs or problems.


Click here to apply online

 

3.) Planning Team Lead

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The Planning Team Lead is the senior leader responsible for all maintenance planning and scheduling on the FPSO (topside, hull, and marine systems). The role combines:
	
		Team leadership of a multidisciplinary group of planners (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, and third-party activities)
		Strategic planning & scheduling (preventive, corrective, and condition-based maintenance)
		CMMS ownership (mainly SAP PM)
		Performance analytics & KPI reporting (using Excel dashboards and Power BI)
		Full HSE, regulatory, and client compliance
	
	
	It is a hands-on leadership role that sits between operations, maintenance execution, and the client, ensuring work is properly planned, resourced, safe, and delivered on time while meeting strict offshore standards


Key Responsibilties
Team Leadership & Coordination:


	Lead, mentor, and supervise a multidisciplinary team of planners covering mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, and third-party maintenance activities.
	Set team objectives, conduct performance reviews, and drive a culture of accountability, precision, and continuous improvement.
	Act as the primary interface between the planning team, operations, maintenance execution, and the client representative.
	Chair daily, weekly, and monthly planning coordination meetings; produce and distribute meeting minutes and action trackers.
	Ensure workload is balanced across the team, providing guidance and resolving planning conflicts proactively.


  Maintenance Planning & Scheduling:


	Develop, maintain, and optimise the Master Maintenance Schedule for all FPSO topside, hull, and marine systems, covering preventive maintenance, corrective maintenance, and condition-based maintenance.
	Prepare and approve detailed Work Packages, including job scope, task breakdowns, resource requirements, material lists, safety isolations, and method statements, in compliance with FPSO operator standards.
	Ensure PM plans are aligned with OEM recommendations, class society requirements, and regulatory inspection schedules (flag state, OCIMF, SIRE).
	Coordinate long-term planning horizons (90-day, 6-month, annual) with short-term weekly and daily schedule management.
	Prioritise work orders in accordance with criticality rankings and asset reliability needs, escalating any conflicts to the Maintenance Manager.
	Manage and coordinate planned shutdown windows and full-field shutdown (FFSD) preparation, including critical path development.


  CMMS Administration & Data Governance:


	Own and administer the CMMS (SAP PM or equivalent) for all planning-related functions: work order creation, scheduling, notifications, equipment history, and backlog management.
	Enforce data quality standards within the CMMS; ensure all completed work orders are closed with accurate feedback, labour hours, materials consumed, and failure codes.
	Audit CMMS data regularly to identify gaps, orphan notifications, overdue PM orders, and incorrect functional locations.
	Drive the development and optimisation of PM task lists, maintenance strategies, and equipment hierarchies within the CMMS.
	Interface with the document control team to ensure all procedures, work instructions, and technical drawings referenced in work packages are current and version-controlled.


  Analytics, KPI Management & Reporting:


	Define, track, analyse, and report on key maintenance performance indicators, including:
	Schedule Compliance (weekly PM and CM execution vs. plan)
	Backlog Management (overdue PMs, open CMs by age and priority)
	Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) by equipment class
	Planned vs. Unplanned Maintenance Ratio
	Maintenance Cost per Discipline vs. budget
	Work Order Cycle Time (notification to close-out)
	Build and maintain dynamic Excel dashboards and Power BI reports to visualise maintenance performance trends for management, clients, and contract reviews.
	Conduct monthly performance analysis and present findings with root cause commentary and corrective action plans.
	Support annual budget preparation by providing detailed maintenance workload forecasts, manpower estimates, and material cost projections.
	Deliver ad-hoc analytical reports as requested by the client or Maintenance Manager, using data drawn from SAP, Excel, and Power BI.


  Regulatory Compliance & Safety:


	Ensure all maintenance plans and work packages comply with applicable offshore regulations, including NUPRC requirements, flag state regulations, OCIMF SIRE, ISM Code, and client HSSE standards.
	Ensure timely scheduling and tracking of safety-critical maintenance (SCM) items, safety valve testing, deluge system verification, and emergency system proof tests.
	Verify that all work packages include appropriate risk assessments, PTW (Permit to Work) references, LOTO plans, and method statements before execution.
	Maintain a live tracker of certification expiry dates for lifting equipment, pressure vessels, safety valves, life-saving appliances, and fire and gas detectors.
	Participate in internal and client HSE audits; implement corrective actions related to the planning function within agreed timescales.


Communication & Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as the planning function’s representative in all client progress meetings, presenting schedule status, KPI performance, and backlog management updates clearly and confidently.
	Translate complex maintenance data into clear, executive-level summaries for monthly contract review meetings.
	Coordinate with procurement and supply chain to ensure materials and spare parts are available ahead of planned maintenance windows; escalate critical spares shortages immediately.
	Liaise with third-party contractors and OEM service providers on scope, mobilisation timelines, and progress reporting


Educational & Job Requirements


	Minimum of 15 years experience in oil and gas maintenance planning, with at least 5 years in an FPSO or offshore floating production environment.
	Proven experience leading a planning team of 4 or more planners across multiple disciplines.
	Expert user of a CMMS platform (SAP PM strongly preferred); able to configure, audit, and train others on the system.
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Power BI for maintenance performance reporting and dashboard development.
	Solid understanding of FPSO maintenance scopes: topside process equipment, rotating machinery, electrical systems, instrumentation, marine systems, lifting equipment, and hull integrity.
	Familiarity with offshore regulatory frameworks: NUPRC, flag state, ISM Code, OCIMF, SIRE, and class society requirements.


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

Key Responsibilities


	Provide high-level administrative support to the General Manager.
	Manage the GM&rsquo;s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel schedules.
	Handle travel logistics including ticketing, hotel bookings, and itinerary coordination.

Executive Assistant Job at MarbleClear Limited




        
    

        
        MarbleClear Limited is a full service Human Resources Management firm committed to optimizing the value of HR within your business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide high-level administrative support to the General Manager.
	Manage the GM’s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel schedules.
	Handle travel logistics including ticketing, hotel bookings, and itinerary coordination.
	Prepare meeting agendas, presentations, reports, and official correspondence.
	Coordinate internal and external meetings, ensuring proper documentation and follow-ups.
	Act as a liaison between the General Manager and internal departments, clients, and external stakeholders.
	Maintain organized records, files, and confidential documents.
	Track tasks and ensure timely follow-up on key action items assigned by the GM.
	Support event coordination, business visits, and executive engagements when required.
	Perform other administrative duties as assigned.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field.
	3–5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a similar administrative role.
	Proven experience handling travel arrangements, ticketing, and booking logistics.
	Strong organizational and time-management skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	High level of professionalism, discretion, and confidentiality.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Ability to multitask and work effectively in a fast-paced environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

IT Engineer Job at Seven-Up Bottling Company Limited




        
    

        
        Seven-Up Bottling Company Ltd is one of the largest manufacturing companies in Nigeria, producing and distributing some of the nation’s most- loved beverages in the country like; Pepsi, 7Up, Mirinda, Teem, Mountain Dew, H2oH!, Lipton Ice Tea and Aquafina premium drinking water. SBC has recently entered into Home & Personal Care category with the 2SURE brand and launched its first product, a Hand & Surface Sanitizer. Seven Up boasts of nine bottling plants with state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities strategically located across various regions in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Engineer

Job Location: Ijora, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Computer Science, Information Technology, Computer Engineering, or a related field.
	2 – 5 years of experience in end-user technical support
	Microsoft Certified Modern Desktop Administrator and Google Platforms.
	ITIL Foundation (For service management environments)
	Strong troubleshooting skills for laptops, desktops, printers, and scanners.
	Hands-on experience with Windows OS installation, configuration, and maintenance.
	Experience managing user accounts in Active Directory
	Basic understanding of networking (LAN, Wi-Fi, DNS, VPN)


Responsibilities


	Provide frontline technical support for laptops, desktops, printers, scanners, and ERP systems to ensure minimal downtime and uninterrupted business operations.
	Install, configure, and maintain end-user devices, including OS, deployment, system upgrades, and hardware troubleshooting.
	Diagnose and resolve software, login, and Active Directory access issues.
	Support network and shared printers, including driver configuration and scan-to-email/folder troubleshooting.
	Provide first-line ERP support, resolving user access issues and transaction error while escalating system bugs when necessary (SAP and other Business Applications)
	Manage user accounts, permissions, and email configuration (Google environment).
	Log and resolve incidents through a helpdesk system, ensuring SLA compliance and user satisfaction.
	Troubleshoot basic network connectivity issues including LAN, Wi-Fi, DNS, and VPN access.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.
